content:
# Numbers Chapter 5 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 152–153; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 114–115 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_005_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_005_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/NUM_005_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_005_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — PURITY AND THE JEALOUSY ORDEAL:** The Leningrad Codex continues on pages 152–153 with three distinct legal units: the expulsion of the unclean from the camp (vv. 1–4), the law of restitution for trespass against a fellow (vv. 5–10), and the elaborate ordeal of the suspected adulteress (sotah) (vv. 11–31). The open parashah mark (פ) is visible before verse 1, and a setumah (ס) division appears between the restitution law and the sotah passage. The chapter is dominated by sacral-legal vocabulary: טָמֵא (tame', "unclean"), מַעַל (ma'al, "trespass/unfaithfulness"), and קִנְאָה (qin'ah, "jealousy/zeal"). The sotah ritual (vv. 11–31) is the longest single legal procedure in the Torah, with its unique elements: the bitter waters (מֵי הַמָּרִים, mei ha-marim), the dust from the Tabernacle floor, the written curses dissolved into water, and the grain offering of jealousy. The Vaticanus LXX (pp. 114–115) follows the MT closely, rendering YHWH as κύριος throughout and providing ὕδωρ τοῦ ἐλεγμοῦ ("water of testing/conviction") for the bitter waters.

---

## Expulsion of the Unclean (vv. 1–4)

**1** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], saying [le'mor]:

**2** "Command [tsav] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el], and-they-shall-send-out [vi-shalekhu] from [min] the-camp [ha-makhaneh] every [kol] leper [tsaru'a] and-every [ve-khol] one-with-a-discharge [zav] and-every [ve-khol] one-unclean [tame'] by-a-soul [la-nafesh].

> **Key terms:** צָרוּעַ (tsaru'a) = "leper" / one afflicted with the skin disease tsara'at (Lev 13–14). זָב (zav) = "one with a discharge" (Lev 15). טָמֵא לָנֶפֶשׁ (tame' la-nafesh) = "unclean by a soul" = one contaminated by contact with a corpse (Num 19). These are the three categories of impurity that require removal from the camp. **LXX:** πάντα λεπρὸν καὶ πάντα γονορρυοῦντα καὶ πάντα ἀκάθαρτον ἐπὶ ψυχῇ — γονορρυοῦντα ("one with a gonorrhea/discharge") for זָב.

**3** From-male [mi-zakhar] unto [ad] female [neqevah] you-shall-send-out [teshalekhu]; unto [el] outside of [mi-khutz] the-camp [la-makhaneh] you-shall-send-them-out [teshalkhum]; and-not [ve-lo'] they-shall-make-unclean [yetamme'u] [et] their-camps [makhaneihem] in-the-midst of-which [asher] I [ani] [am]-dwelling [shokhen] in-their-midst [be-tokham]."

> **Key terms:** שֹׁכֵן בְּתוֹכָם (shokhen be-tokham) = "dwelling in their midst" — the theological ground for the expulsion: YHWH's indwelling presence requires the camp's purity. The plural מַחֲנֵיהֶם (makhaneihem, "their camps") may reflect the tripartite camp structure (Levitical, priestly, Israelite). **LXX:** ἐν οἷς ἐγὼ καταγίνομαι ἐν αὐτοῖς ("in which I dwell among them").

**4** And-did [va-ya'asu] so [ken] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el], and-they-sent-them-out [va-yeshalekhu] them [otam] unto [el] outside of [mi-khutz] the-camp [la-makhaneh]; as-which [ka'asher] spoke [dibber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], so [ken] did [asu] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el].

> **Key terms:** The obedience formula is doubled: כַּאֲשֶׁר דִּבֶּר יְהוָה ... כֵּן עָשׂוּ ("as YHWH spoke ... so they did") — echoing the construction compliance formulas of Exodus 39–40. **LXX:** καθὰ ἐλάλησεν κύριος τῷ Μωυσῇ, οὕτως ἐποίησαν — identical structure.

---

## Restitution for Trespass (vv. 5–10)

**5** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], saying [le'mor]:

**6** "Speak [dabber] unto [el] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el]: A-man [ish] or [o] a-woman [ishah], when [ki] they-do [ya'asu] any-of [mi-kol] the-sins of [khatot] the-adam [ha-adam] to-commit-a-trespass [lim'ol] a-trespass [ma'al] against-YHWH [ba-YHWH], and-is-guilty [ve-ashmah] that-soul [ha-nefesh ha-hi'] —

> **Key terms:** מַעַל (ma'al) = "trespass/unfaithfulness" — a sacral term for breach of faith, here applied to wrongs against a fellow human being that are simultaneously wrongs against YHWH. חַטֹּאת הָאָדָם (khatot ha-adam) = "the sins of the-adam/humankind" — the genitive is ambiguous: sins characteristic of human beings, or sins committed against other humans. **LXX:** ἁμαρτίαν ... πλημμελῆσαι πλημμέλειαν ("to commit a trespass-offering's offense") — πλημμέλεια normally renders אָשָׁם.

**7** And-they-shall-confess [ve-hitvaddu] [et] their-sin [khatta'tam] which [asher] they-did [asu], and-he-shall-return [ve-heshiv] [et] his-guilt [ashamo] in-its-head [be-ro'sho], and-its-fifth [va-khamishito] he-shall-add [yosef] upon-it [alav], and-he-shall-give [ve-natan] to-whom [la-asher] he-was-guilty [asham] against-him [lo].

> **Key terms:** וְהִתְוַדּוּ (ve-hitvaddu) = "and they shall confess" — the hitpa'el of ידה (yadah, "to acknowledge/confess"), one of only a few places in the Torah where verbal confession is explicitly commanded. בְּרֹאשׁוֹ (be-ro'sho) = "in its head" = the principal amount. חֲמִישִׁתוֹ (khamishito) = "its fifth" — a twenty-percent surcharge added to the restitution, identical to the requirement in Lev 5:24 [6:5]. **LXX:** τὴν κεφαλὴν τῆς πλημμελείας ("the head/principal of the trespass") + τὸ ἐπίπεμπτον ("the one-fifth").

**8** And-if [ve-im] there-is-not [ein] to-the-man [la-ish] a-kinsman-redeemer [go'el] to-return [le-hashiv] the-guilt [ha-asham] to-him [elav], the-guilt [ha-asham] the-returned [ha-mushav] to-YHWH [la-YHWH] [shall be] for-the-priest [la-kohen], apart-from [mi-levad] the-ram of [eil] the-atonements [ha-kippurim] which [asher] he-shall-make-atonement [yekhapper] upon-him [alav] with-it [bo].

> **Key terms:** גֹּאֵל (go'el) = "kinsman-redeemer" — the nearest relative responsible for receiving restitution on behalf of a deceased person. If no go'el exists, the restitution goes to YHWH via the priest. אֵיל הַכִּפֻּרִים (eil ha-kippurim) = "the ram of the atonements" — the sacrifice that accompanies monetary restitution (cf. Lev 5:15–16). **LXX:** ἀγχιστεύων ("one who is next of kin") for גֹּאֵל; κριὸς τοῦ ἱλασμοῦ ("ram of propitiation") for אֵיל הַכִּפֻּרִים.

**9** And-every [ve-khol] contribution [terumah] of-all [le-khol] the-holy-things of [qodshei] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el] which [asher] they-bring-near [yaqrivu] to-the-priest [la-kohen], to-him [lo] it-shall-be [yihyeh].

> **Key terms:** תְּרוּמָה (terumah) = "contribution/heave-offering" — the portion lifted/set apart for YHWH. This verse broadens beyond the specific restitution case to establish the general principle of priestly entitlement to sacred donations. **LXX:** ἀφαίρεμα ("that which is taken away/set apart") for תְּרוּמָה.

**10** And-a-man's [ve-ish] holy-things [qodashav], to-him [lo] they-shall-be [yihyu]; a-man [ish] who [asher] gives [yitten] to-the-priest [la-kohen], to-him [lo] it-shall-be [yihyeh]."

> **Key terms:** The repetition of לוֹ יִהְיֶה ("to him it shall be") twice in vv. 9–10 reinforces the principle: once holy things are given to the priest, they become the priest's property. The verse is ambiguous — it may mean "each man's holy things are his [to give]" or "whatever a man gives to the priest becomes his [the priest's]." Both readings are grammatically valid; the latter is favored by context. **LXX:** follows MT closely: ἑκάστου τὰ ἅγια αὐτοῦ, αὐτῷ ἔσται ("each one's holy things, to him they shall be").

---

## The Ordeal of Jealousy — The Sotah (vv. 11–31)

**11** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], saying [le'mor]:

**12** "Speak [dabber] unto [el] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el], and-you-shall-say [ve-amarta] unto [alehem] them: A-man [ish], a-man [ish], when [ki] turns-aside [tisteh] his-wife [ishto] and-she-commits-a-trespass [u-ma'alah] against-him [bo] a-trespass [ma'al],

> **Key terms:** תִשְׂטֶה (tisteh) = "turns aside/goes astray" — from the root שׂטה (satah), the very root from which the term סוֹטָה (sotah, "the wayward woman") derives. The doubling אִישׁ אִישׁ (ish ish, "a man, a man") is a distributive idiom: "any man whatsoever." מַעַל (ma'al) = "trespass" — the same term used in v. 6 for wrongs against a fellow; here applied to marital unfaithfulness. **LXX:** ἐὰν παραβῇ αὐτοῦ ἡ γυνὴ ("if his wife transgresses against him") — παραβῇ for תִשְׂטֶה.

**13** And-lies [ve-shakhav] a-man [ish] with-her [otah] [with]-a-lying of [shikevat] seed [zera'], and-it-is-hidden [ve-ne'lam] from-the-eyes of [me-einei] her-husband [ishah], and-she-is-concealed [ve-nisterah], and-she [ve-hi'] has-become-defiled [nitma'ah], and-[there is]-no [ve-ein] witness [ed] against-her [bah], and-she [ve-hi'] was-not [lo'] caught [nitpasah] —

> **Key terms:** שִׁכְבַת־זֶרַע (shikhvat-zera') = "a lying of seed" = sexual intercourse. The phrase נֶעְלַם מֵעֵינֵי (ne'lam me-einei, "hidden from the eyes of") establishes the evidentiary gap that necessitates the ordeal: there is no direct proof. נִתְפָּשָׂה (nitpasah) = "caught/seized" — the niphal of תָּפַשׂ. **LXX:** λάθῃ ... ἐξ ὀφθαλμῶν τοῦ ἀνδρὸς αὐτῆς ("escapes notice ... from the eyes of her husband").

**14** And-comes [ve-avar] upon-him [alav] a-spirit of [ru'akh] jealousy [qin'ah], and-he-is-jealous-of [ve-qinne'] [et] his-wife [ishto], and-she [ve-hi'] has-become-defiled [nitma'ah]; or [o] [there]-comes [avar] upon-him [alav] a-spirit of [ru'akh] jealousy [qin'ah], and-he-is-jealous-of [ve-qinne'] [et] his-wife [ishto], and-she [ve-hi'] has-not [lo'] become-defiled [nitma'ah] —

> **Key terms:** רוּחַ קִנְאָה (ru'akh qin'ah) = "a spirit of jealousy/zeal" — the procedure applies whether or not the woman is actually guilty. The doubled scenario (she is defiled / she is not defiled) establishes that the ordeal functions in both directions: to convict the guilty and to vindicate the innocent. קִנְאָה (qin'ah) derives from the root קנא, which encompasses both jealousy and righteous zeal (cf. Num 25:11). **LXX:** πνεῦμα ζηλώσεως ("a spirit of jealousy/emulation").

**15** And-shall-bring [ve-hevi'] the-man [ha-ish] [et] his-wife [ishto] unto [el] the-priest [ha-kohen], and-he-shall-bring [ve-hevi'] [et] her-offering [qorbanah] upon-her [aleha]: a-tenth of [asirit] the-ephah [ha-eifah] [of]-barley [se'orim] flour [qemakh]; he-shall-not [lo'] pour [yitsoq] upon-it [alav] oil [shemen], and-not [ve-lo'] shall-he-put [yitten] upon-it [alav] frankincense [levonah], for [ki] it [is] a-grain-offering of [minkhat] jealousies [qena'ot], a-grain-offering of [minkhat] remembrance [zikkaron], bringing-to-remembrance [mazkeret] iniquity [avon].

> **Key terms:** שְׂעֹרִים (se'orim) = "barley" — the only grain offering in the Torah made from barley rather than wheat; barley was considered a lesser grain, possibly reflecting the degraded state of suspicion. No oil or frankincense — these elements of joy and honor (Lev 2:1) are omitted because this is an offering of accusation, not celebration. מִנְחַת קְנָאֹת (minkhat qena'ot) = "a grain offering of jealousies." מַזְכֶּרֶת עָוֹן (mazkeret avon) = "bringing iniquity to remembrance" — the offering functions as a memorial that summons hidden guilt before YHWH. **LXX:** θυσία ζηλοτυπίας, θυσία μνημοσύνου ἀναμιμνῄσκουσα ἁμαρτίαν — ζηλοτυπία ("jealousy") and μνημόσυνον ("memorial") render the Hebrew precisely.

**16** And-shall-bring-her-near [ve-hiqriv] the-priest [ha-kohen] [otah], and-he-shall-set-her [ve-he'emidah] before [lifnei] YHWH.

**17** And-shall-take [ve-laqakh] the-priest [ha-kohen] holy [qedoshim] water [mayim] in-a-vessel of [bi-kheli] earthenware [khares], and-from [u-min] the-dust [he-afar] which [asher] is [yihyeh] on-the-floor of [be-qarqa'] the-Tabernacle [ha-Mishkan] the-priest [ha-kohen] shall-take [yiqqakh] and-put [ve-natan] into [el] the-water [ha-mayim].

> **Key terms:** מַיִם קְדֹשִׁים (mayim qedoshim) = "holy water" — water set apart, likely from the laver. כְּלִי חֶרֶשׂ (keli khares) = "a vessel of earthenware" — clay pots, being porous, could not be purified and were therefore disposable, suitable for a one-time ritual. קַרְקַע הַמִּשְׁכָּן (qarqa' ha-Mishkan) = "the floor of the Tabernacle" — the dust from sacred ground is mixed into the water, making it a kind of sacramental substance charged with holy-space power. **LXX:** ὕδωρ καθαρὸν ζῶν ("clean living water") for מַיִם קְדֹשִׁים — an interpretive expansion; χοῦν ("dust/earth") for עָפָר.

**18** And-shall-stand [ve-he'emid] the-priest [ha-kohen] [et] the-woman [ha-ishah] before [lifnei] YHWH, and-he-shall-uncover [u-fara'] [et] the-head of [ro'sh] the-woman [ha-ishah], and-he-shall-put [ve-natan] upon [al] her-palms [kappeyha] [et] the-grain-offering of [minkhat] the-remembrance [ha-zikkaron] — it [hi'] [is] a-grain-offering of [minkhat] jealousies [qena'ot]; and-in-the-hand of [u-ve-yad] the-priest [ha-kohen] shall-be [yihyu] the-waters of [mei] the-bitters [ha-marim] the-cursing [ha-me'ar'rim].

> **Key terms:** וּפָרַע (u-fara') = "and he shall uncover/dishevel" — loosening the woman's hair, a sign of mourning and exposure (cf. Lev 13:45; 21:10). מֵי הַמָּרִים הַמְאָרֲרִים (mei ha-marim ha-me'ar'rim) = "the waters of the bitters that curse" — a double wordplay: מָרִים (marim) = "bitter" and מְאָרֲרִים (me'ar'rim) = "that bring a curse," from the root אָרַר (arar). **LXX:** τὸ ὕδωρ τοῦ ἐλεγμοῦ τοῦ ἐπικαταρωμένου ("the water of conviction that is accursed") — ἐλεγμός ("testing/conviction") interprets rather than translates מָרִים.

**19** And-shall-make-swear [ve-hishbi'a] her [otah] the-priest [ha-kohen], and-he-shall-say [ve-amar] unto [el] the-woman [ha-ishah]: 'If [im] not [lo'] lay [shakhav] a-man [ish] with-you [otakh], and-if [ve-im] not [lo'] you-turned-aside [satit] [to]-uncleanness [tum'ah] under [takhat] your-husband [ishekh], be-free [hinnqi] from-the-waters of [mi-mei] the-bitters [ha-marim] the-cursing [ha-me'ar'rim] the-these [ha-elleh].

> **Key terms:** הִנָּקִי (hinnqi) = "be free/be clean" — the niphal imperative of נקה (naqah, "to be clean/innocent"). The oath begins with the case of innocence: if she has not strayed, she shall be unharmed by the water. תַּחַת אִישֵׁךְ (takhat ishekh) = "under your husband" = under the authority of your husband — the marital relationship expressed as a hierarchical bond. **LXX:** ἀθῴα ἴσθι ("be acquitted") for הִנָּקִי.

**20** And-you [ve-at], if [ki] you-turned-aside [satit] under [takhat] your-husband [ishekh], and-if [ve-khi] you-became-defiled [nitme't], and-gave [va-yitten] a-man [ish] in-you [bakh] [et] his-lying [shekhavto] apart-from [mi-bal'adei] your-husband [ishekh]' —"

**21** And-shall-make-swear [ve-hishbi'a] the-priest [ha-kohen] [et] the-woman [ha-ishah] with-the-oath of [bi-shevu'at] the-curse [ha-alah], and-shall-say [ve-amar] the-priest [ha-kohen] to-the-woman [la-ishah]: "May-give [yitten] YHWH you [otakh] for-a-curse [le-alah] and-for-an-oath [ve-li-shevu'ah] in-the-midst of [be-tokh] your-people [ammekh], in-the-giving of [be-tet] YHWH [et] your-thigh [yerekekh] falling [nofelet] and [ve-et] your-belly [bitneikh] swelling [tsavah].

> **Key terms:** אָלָה (alah) = "curse/imprecation" — the sworn curse that will take effect if the woman is guilty. יְרֵכֵךְ נֹפֶלֶת (yerekekh nofelet) = "your thigh falling/wasting" — יָרֵךְ (yarekh) often euphemistically refers to the reproductive organs (cf. Gen 46:26). בִּטְנֵךְ צָבָה (bitneikh tsavah) = "your belly swelling" — the physical symptoms of the curse suggest some form of reproductive affliction. The phrase לְאָלָה וְלִשְׁבֻעָה (le-alah ve-li-shevu'ah) = "for a curse and for an oath" means the woman's fate will become a proverbial example invoked in oaths. **LXX:** τὸν μηρόν σου διαπεπτωκότα καὶ τὴν κοιλίαν σου πεπρησμένην ("your thigh fallen away and your belly swollen").

**22** And-shall-come [u-va'u] the-waters [ha-mayim] the-cursing [ha-me'ar'rim] the-these [ha-elleh] into-your-inward-parts [be-me'ayikh], to-cause-to-swell [la-tsebot] [the]-belly [beten] and-to-cause-to-fall [u-le-nappil] [the]-thigh [yarekh]." And-shall-say [ve-amerah] the-woman [ha-ishah]: "Amen [amen], amen [amen]."

> **Key terms:** אָמֵן אָמֵן (amen, amen) = "truly, truly" / "so be it, so be it" — the woman's verbal assent to the oath-curse. This is the first occurrence of the doubled amen in the Hebrew Bible. The word derives from the root אמן ('aman, "to be firm/faithful/true") — the same root as אֱמֶת (emet, "truth"). **LXX:** γένοιτο, γένοιτο ("may it be, may it be") — the optative mood captures the assent.

**23** And-shall-write [ve-khatav] the-priest [ha-kohen] [et] these [ha-elleh] curses [ha-alot] in-a-scroll [ba-sefer], and-he-shall-blot-out [u-makhah] into [el] the-waters of [mei] the-bitters [ha-marim].

> **Key terms:** סֵפֶר (sefer) = "scroll/document." וּמָחָה (u-makhah) = "and he shall blot out/dissolve" — the written curses are physically washed off the scroll into the water, so that the woman literally drinks the words. This is the only Torah ritual that involves the destruction of written text as part of the procedure. **LXX:** ἐξαλείψει ("he shall wipe out/erase") for וּמָחָה.

**24** And-he-shall-cause-to-drink [ve-hishqah] [et] the-woman [ha-ishah] [et] the-waters of [mei] the-bitters [ha-marim] the-cursing [ha-me'ar'rim], and-shall-come [u-va'u] into-her [bah] the-waters [ha-mayim] the-cursing [ha-me'ar'rim] for-bitters [le-marim].

> **Key terms:** The phrase לְמָרִים (le-marim, "for bitters/bitterness") at the end restates the effect: the cursing waters will become bitter within her — that is, they will bring bitter consequences if she is guilty. The threefold repetition of הַמָּרִים הַמְאָרֲרִים in vv. 18, 19, 24 drives home the gravity of the ritual. **LXX:** τὸ ὕδωρ τοῦ ἐλεγμοῦ τοῦ ἐπικαταρωμένου — consistent rendering.

**25** And-shall-take [ve-laqakh] the-priest [ha-kohen] from-the-hand of [mi-yad] the-woman [ha-ishah] [et] the-grain-offering of [minkhat] the-jealousies [ha-qena'ot], and-he-shall-wave [ve-henif] [et] the-grain-offering [ha-minkhah] before [lifnei] YHWH, and-he-shall-bring-near [ve-hiqriv] her [otah] unto [el] the-altar [ha-mizbe'akh].

> **Key terms:** וְהֵנִיף (ve-henif) = "and he shall wave" — the tenufah wave offering, moving the offering before YHWH as a presentation gesture before placing it on the altar. **LXX:** ἐπιθήσει ... ἐπὶ τὸ θυσιαστήριον ("he shall place ... upon the altar").

**26** And-shall-take-a-handful [ve-qamats] the-priest [ha-kohen] from [min] the-grain-offering [ha-minkhah] [et] its-memorial-portion [azkaratah], and-he-shall-burn [ve-hiqtir] [it] on-the-altar [ha-mizbkhah], and-afterward [ve-akhar] he-shall-cause-to-drink [yashqeh] [et] the-woman [ha-ishah] [et] the-water [ha-mayim].

> **Key terms:** אַזְכָּרָתָהּ (azkaratah) = "its memorial portion" — the qomets (handful) that is burned on the altar as a representative portion (cf. Lev 2:2, 9, 16). The sequence is significant: the memorial portion is burned first, then the woman drinks — the offering ascends to YHWH before the ordeal takes effect. **LXX:** τὸ μνημόσυνον αὐτῆς ("its memorial").

**27** And-he-shall-cause-her-to-drink [ve-hishqah] [et] the-water [ha-mayim], and-it-shall-be [ve-hayah], if [im] she-has-become-defiled [nitme'ah] and-she-committed-a-trespass [va-tim'ol] a-trespass [ma'al] against-her-husband [be-ishah], and-shall-come [u-va'u] into-her [bah] the-waters [ha-mayim] the-cursing [ha-me'ar'rim] for-bitters [le-marim], and-shall-swell [ve-tsavetah] her-belly [bitna], and-shall-fall [ve-naflah] her-thigh [yerekhah]; and-shall-become [ve-hayetah] the-woman [ha-ishah] for-a-curse [le-alah] in-the-midst of [be-qerev] her-people [ammah].

> **Key terms:** The curse effects are now stated as consequences: צָבְתָה בִטְנָהּ (tsavetah bitnah, "her belly shall swell") and נָפְלָה יְרֵכָהּ (naflah yerekhah, "her thigh shall fall"). The woman becomes לְאָלָה (le-alah) = "a [byword of] curse" among her people — her name will be invoked in future imprecations. **LXX:** εἰς ἀρὰν ἐν μέσῳ τοῦ λαοῦ αὐτῆς ("for a curse in the midst of her people").

**28** And-if [ve-im] has-not [lo'] become-defiled [nitme'ah] the-woman [ha-ishah], and-clean [u-tehorah] she [hi'], and-she-shall-be-free [ve-niqqetah], and-she-shall-be-sown [ve-nizre'ah] with-seed [zara'].

> **Key terms:** וְנִזְרְעָה זָרַע (ve-nizre'ah zara') = "and she shall be sown [with] seed" = she shall conceive children. The niphal of זרע ("to sow") used of a woman implies that vindication through the ordeal will be followed by fertility — a positive counter to the curse of reproductive failure. וְנִקְּתָה (ve-niqqetah) = "and she shall be free/innocent" — full exoneration. **LXX:** καὶ σπερεῖ σπέρμα ("and she shall sow seed") — identical idiom.

**29** This [zo't] [is] the-law of [torat] the-jealousies [ha-qena'ot], when [asher] turns-aside [tisteh] a-wife [ishah] under [takhat] her-husband [ishah] and-she-becomes-defiled [ve-nitma'ah],

**30** or [o] a-man [ish] whom [asher] passes [ta'avor] upon-him [alav] a-spirit of [ru'akh] jealousy [qin'ah], and-he-is-jealous-of [ve-qinne'] [et] his-wife [ishto]; and-he-shall-stand [ve-he'emid] [et] the-woman [ha-ishah] before [lifnei] YHWH, and-the-priest [ha-kohen] shall-do [ve-asah] to-her [lah] [et] all [kol] this [ha-torah ha-zo't] law [ha-zo't].

> **Key terms:** תּוֹרַת הַקְּנָאֹת (torat ha-qena'ot) = "the law/instruction of the jealousies" — the summary title for the entire procedure. The formulation echoes other תּוֹרַת ("law of") headings (cf. Lev 6–7). **LXX:** ὁ νόμος τῆς ζηλοτυπίας ("the law of jealousy").

**31** And-shall-be-free [ve-niqqah] the-man [ha-ish] from-iniquity [me-avon], and-the-woman [ve-ha-ishah] the-that [ha-hi'] shall-bear [tissa'] [et] her-iniquity [avonah]."

> **Key terms:** וְנִקָּה הָאִישׁ מֵעָוֹן (ve-niqqah ha-ish me-avon) = "and the man shall be free from iniquity" — even if the ordeal proves the woman innocent, the husband bears no guilt for having subjected her to it; his jealousy (whether justified or not) is not punished. תִּשָּׂא אֶת עֲוֹנָהּ (tissa' et avonah) = "she shall bear her iniquity" — if guilty, the consequences fall upon her alone. **LXX:** ἀθῷος ἔσται ὁ ἄνθρωπος ἀπὸ ἁμαρτίας ("the man shall be innocent from sin").

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Three-tiered purity and camp topology (vv. 1–4):** The three categories of impurity — tsara'at (skin disease), zav (bodily discharge), and corpse contamination — correspond to three graduated zones of exclusion in rabbinic interpretation (b. Pesakhim 67a): the metsora' is expelled from all three camps; the zav from two (Levitical and Israelite); the corpse-defiled from one (the camp of the Divine Presence). The MT does not make these distinctions explicit here, but the Levitical legislation in Leviticus 13–15 and Numbers 19 supports the graduated reading. The Leningrad Codex text on page 152 reads all three terms clearly in verse 2.

2. **מַעַל (ma'al) as a bridging concept (vv. 6, 12):** The term מַעַל ("trespass/unfaithfulness") appears in both the restitution law (v. 6, for wrongs against a neighbor that are "against YHWH") and the sotah law (v. 12, for marital unfaithfulness). This lexical linkage binds the chapter's seemingly disparate sections: all forms of hidden betrayal — financial and sexual — constitute sacral trespass. The root מעל in the Hebrew Bible always implies breach of a trust relationship, whether with YHWH or with a covenant partner.

3. **The barley offering and its significance (v. 15):** The sotah's grain offering is uniquely specified as barley flour (קֶמַח שְׂעֹרִים, qemakh se'orim) rather than the standard fine wheat flour (סֹלֶת, solet) used in all other grain offerings (Lev 2:1). The Mishnah (Sotah 2:1) explains: "Her offering is of barley — as her deeds are the deeds of an animal, so her offering is animal food." Whether or not one accepts this moral reading, the ritual distinction is deliberate: the absence of oil and frankincense, combined with the inferior grain, marks this offering as one of judgment rather than worship.

4. **מֵי הַמָּרִים הַמְאָרֲרִים — the bitter waters (vv. 18, 19, 23, 24, 27):** The phrase involves a wordplay on two distinct roots: מָרִים (marim) from מרר (marar, "to be bitter") and מְאָרֲרִים (me'ar'rim) from אָרַר (arar, "to curse"). The Leningrad Codex consistently vocalizes both elements with clear Masoretic pointing. The LXX Vaticanus renders the phrase as ὕδωρ τοῦ ἐλεγμοῦ τοῦ ἐπικαταρωμένου ("water of the conviction that is accursed"), interpreting מָרִים as "testing/conviction" (ἐλεγμός) rather than "bitter" — a significant exegetical choice that emphasizes the forensic rather than the physical dimension of the ritual.

5. **The sequence of the ritual (vv. 16–27):** The ordeal proceeds in a precisely choreographed order: (a) the woman is positioned before YHWH (v. 16); (b) the holy water and dust are prepared (v. 17); (c) her hair is uncovered and the grain offering placed in her hands (v. 18); (d) the priest administers the oath (vv. 19–22); (e) the woman assents with "amen, amen" (v. 22); (f) the curses are written and dissolved into the water (v. 23); (g) she drinks the water (v. 24 — though v. 26 suggests this may occur after the memorial burning); (h) the grain offering is waved and the memorial portion burned (vv. 25–26); (i) the water takes effect (v. 27). There is an apparent doublet in the drinking command (vv. 24 and 26), which some scholars attribute to redactional layering. The EMET translation preserves both without harmonization, following the Leningrad Codex text as it stands.

6. **וְנִזְרְעָה זָרַע — "she shall be sown with seed" (v. 28):** This phrase is the sole positive outcome described for the innocent woman: fertility as divine vindication. The niphal passive (she is sown, not she sows) implies that conception is a gift from YHWH, not merely a biological outcome. The LXX renders this with the active σπερεῖ σπέρμα ("she shall sow seed"), subtly shifting agency to the woman. Some Qumran fragments and the Samaritan Pentateuch read זֶרַע with the article (הַזֶּרַע), but the Leningrad Codex has the unadorned form.

7. **The husband's immunity (v. 31):** The final verse grants the husband blanket immunity: וְנִקָּה הָאִישׁ מֵעָוֹן ("the man shall be free from iniquity"). This asymmetry — the husband faces no consequences whether the accusation proves true or false — has been the subject of extensive ethical and legal discussion. The verse may function as a legal protection against counter-litigation: a husband who brings his wife to the ordeal in good faith cannot be sued for slander if she is vindicated. The LXX renders this straightforwardly without expansion.

8. **LXX Vaticanus distinctive readings (pp. 114–115):** The Vaticanus text shows the following noteworthy divergences from the MT: (a) ὕδωρ καθαρὸν ζῶν ("clean living water") for מַיִם קְדֹשִׁים ("holy water") in v. 17 — "living" (flowing) water may reflect a different Vorlage or an interpretive gloss; (b) ἐλεγμός ("conviction/testing") consistently for מָרִים ("bitter") throughout the sotah passage; (c) minor word-order variations in the oath formulae (vv. 19–22) that do not affect meaning. Overall, the LXX of Numbers 5 demonstrates a translator comfortable with interpretive renderings of cultic terminology while maintaining close structural fidelity to the Hebrew.
